title: Support EVCD $dumpports
body:
Hello guys,

i was using verilator for dumping some traces (VCDs).

i found out that is does not support the following VCD format 

```
....
$var port [31:0] <0  a $end
$var port 1 <1  b $end
....

#1
0<1
00000000000000000000000000<0
```

I don't know if it is an accepted widely used standards, we (at University) are using this format (from another logic simulator) and we developed a toolchain for evaluating the toggle activity/coverage of signals.


Moreover, another format used is the following (mainly used from fault simulators, as far as i knwo) is the eVCD (extended VCD):
Signals in the upscopes are declared as the previous format.
```
pD 6 0 <0
pDDDNNDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDNDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD 6666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666666 0006600000000000000000000000000000060000000000000000000000000000 <4
pFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFDDDUDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD 0000000000000000000000000000000066606666666666666666666666666666 0000000000000000000000000000000000060000000000000000000000000000 <5
pD 6 0 <6
pLLLLLLLH 66666660 00000006 <9
```
Where, for example:
- **p** represents port change 
- **H** output high 
- **0** zero drive strength (no drive)  to **7** drive strength (pull) as verilog implements
- **U**  Input high(up)

If i am not wrong, verilator supports signals strength. I do not know if it could be interesting to implement a dump of an eVCD.

I already implemented a custom ```verilated_vcd_c.cpp``` for dumping the first format, if you need it.

Cheers 😄 😈
